description: Elon Musk's $44 billion acquisition of Twitter in 2022 polarized the public, with supporters citing free speech absolutism and opponents fearing platform deregulation. The deal closed in October 2022, causing massive internal turnover and public scrutiny.

key_events.description: Musk announces initial 9.2 percent stake purchase.
Musk threatens to pull out of the $44B deal due to bot concerns.
Musk completes acquisition and fires top executives.
Massive advertiser exodus following platform policy changes.

calibration_notes: Useful for assessing volatility in opinion when a high-profile individual influences corporate governance.
